diffSummary: Introduced the missing `session` layer (ADR-0003 order types→config→store→zalo→session→command). Added src-tauri/src/session/mod.rs: a SessionManager that owns N concurrent per-account sessions keyed by AccountId, each pairing the authenticated API handle with its live realtime listener. Lifecycle: insert() treats a re-login as a restart and gracefully stops the previous listener (via Listener::stop) before replacing it; remove() stops the listener and clears the account's pacing state; get()/account_ids()/count() expose the set. A per-account SendThrottle spaces outbound messages by at least DEFAULT_SEND_INTERVAL (800ms) so the client cannot behave spam-like (project risk r1). The realtime listener is abstracted behind a MessageListener trait (implemented for zalo::Listener) so lifecycle logic is unit-testable and zca-rust's concrete type stays confined to the zalo layer. command/mod.rs: the in-command ListenerState { HashMap<AccountId, Arc<API>> + unbounded Vec<Listener> } is replaced by managed SessionState(SessionManager); login/QR/restore/file paths register via SessionManager::insert; send_message delegates to SessionManager::send_text (throttled); list_contacts/list_groups resolve the API via SessionManager::get. lib.rs manages SessionState::default(). The UI IPC surface is unchanged (the multi-account rail/routing in src/lib/session.svelte.ts already existed). 6 new offline tests cover replace-stops-old-listener, remove, multi-account tracking, and throttle pacing; a #[ignore] two_accounts_live smoke logs in two real accounts and proves both receive their own self-listen marker tagged with the correct account id (no cross-account leak) and that removal stops both listeners.

checks.name: rust.build
checks.summary: cargo build compiles the new session layer + command refactor (SessionManager replaces ListenerState).

checks.name: lint
checks.summary: cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean for the session layer + command refactor + live test.

checks.name: tests
checks.summary: cargo test: 29 passed offline incl. 6 new session-layer tests (replace stops old listener, remove, multi-account tracking, throttle pacing); 9 live tests ignored by default.

checks.name: structural
checks.summary: npm run harness:check no-op pass (structuralTest.engine=none per ADR-0003); layer order enforced by config domains[].layers.

checks.name: smoke
checks.summary: Live: real account logged in, registered in SessionManager, sent ONE message via the throttled send_text path to the authorized recipient (resolved by phone); the manager-owned listener surfaced the marker tagged with the correct account id; remove() stopped the listener (count 1 -> 0). Non-secret diagnostics only.

knownRisks.description: Per-account send rate limiting was missing; bulk/automated sends could look spam-like and risk an account ban (project risk r1).
knownRisks.acceptanceReason: Resolved by the session layer's per-account SendThrottle (DEFAULT_SEND_INTERVAL=800ms), exercised by offline tests and applied on the send_message path.
knownRisks.mitigation: SessionManager::send_text reserves a per-account slot and waits before sending; different accounts are paced independently.
